reasoning: Polar vloxes (pointy ears) are 'more mellivu'. Vlox I is the most visually prominent and largest, thus 'more mellivu'. Vlox A is a representative of the standard pointy-eared vloxes, making it a suitable second 'mellivu' vlox.

Given 'mellivu' refers to polar (pointy-eared) vloxes, and the manager asks for 'mellovu' vloxes, 'mellovu' likely refers to the contrasting breed, valley (rounded-eared) vloxes. The note indicates 'mellovu' is not a comparative trait, but it can be an absolute descriptor. Vloxes B, C, E, F are rounded-eared. E and F are assigned to other categories, leaving B and C as the two remaining rounded-eared vloxes.

'Kibb' is a trait found in 'imxo' (common) vloxes. The medium, pointy-eared vloxes (A, D, G, H) represent common types. G and H are assigned elsewhere. A and D are the remaining two vloxes from this group available to be 'kibb' vloxes.

The song 'Lookin' Like a Nearly Azeltorf Vlox' implies 'Azeltorf' is a distinctive and desirable look. Vlox I, being the largest and most unique, is 'nearly Azeltorf'. Vlox A, as a prominent medium-sized pointy-eared vlox, is a suitable second distinctive vlox, also being one of the 'mellivu' vloxes with I.
'Qliqli' refers to young vloxes, which are small. Vloxes B, C, E, F are the small, rounded-eared vloxes. As the question asks for one, E is an arbitrary choice from this group.
'Imxo' means common or typical. The medium, pointy-eared vloxes (A, D, G, H) are the most typical. H is assigned elsewhere. G is an arbitrary choice from the remaining typical vloxes.

'Ravv' is a common trait found in 'imxo' vloxes. The medium, pointy-eared vloxes (A, D, G, H) are suitable candidates. G is already identified as 'imxo'. A and D are also common types, available for this trait, forming a group of three with G.

'Kibbish-zuff' is associated with a modern, important vlox (the Prime Minister's vlox). The medium, pointy-eared vloxes (A, D, G, H) represent modern/mature types. H is an arbitrary choice from this group.

'Zuffish-kibb' is a trait of prehistoric vloxes. Young/small vloxes (B, C, E, F) with rounded ears might represent more primitive forms. E is assigned elsewhere. F is an arbitrary choice from the remaining small, rounded-eared vloxes.

The youngest vlox is 'qliqli'. The article states 'prehistoric vloxes were zuffish-kibb or tezzish-kibb'. It's a common biological concept that juvenile forms can resemble ancestral forms. The structure '(a) ish-(b)' matches 'zuffish-kibb'. Thus, 'zuff' for (a) and 'kibb' for (b), using two different words as required.
